The object is still at cols 24-25 (N) and 26-29 (c). It didn't move! The last right move didn't work. The object is stuck again.
Let me think about this. The object is at cols 24-25, and the target is at cols 28-29. The gap is 4 columns (1 move). But the object can't move further right.
Wait, looking at the charcoal part: it's at cols 26-29. The target N is at cols 28-29. So the charcoal part overlaps with the target position! Maybe the charcoal part is blocking the move because it would overlap with the fixed green segment on the bar.
Actually, looking more carefully at the layout:
- The black bar is at rows 40-43
- The fixed green segment on the bar is at cols 28-29
- The movable object is at rows 52-57, below the bar
So the movable object and the bar are on different rows. The charcoal part of the movable object is at cols 26-29, which is below the bar's green segment at cols 28-29. They're vertically separated, so they shouldn't overlap.
But maybe the game considers the vertical alignment. The movable object's N part needs to align vertically with the bar's N segment. Currently:
- Bar's N: rows 40-43, cols 28-29
- Movable N: rows 52-57, cols 24-25
The N parts are not aligned horizontally (24-25 vs 28-29). If I move right by 4, the movable N would be at cols 28-29, aligned with the bar's N. But the move is blocked.
Let me think about why. Maybe the white region's boundary is the issue. Let me check the white region again.
Actually, wait. Let me reconsider the game mechanics. In level 1, the object moved 4 columns per click. In level 2, the same seems to be true. But the object is now stuck at cols 24-25.
Let me check if the white region's right boundary is at col 25 or so. If the white region ends at col 25, then the object's N part at cols 24-25 is at the edge, and moving right would put it at cols 28-29, which is outside the white region.
But wait, the charcoal part is already at cols 26-29, which is outside the white region (in the gray area). So the object is already partially in the gray area.
Hmm, maybe the rule is that the N (green) part must stay within the white region? If the white region ends at col 25, then the N part at cols 24-25 is at the edge, and moving right would put the N part at cols 28-29, which is in the gray area. That would violate the rule.
So maybe I need to expand the white region first using the upper blue blocks. Let me try clicking the blue block at rows 24-27 (between the bars) to see if it expands the white region.
Actually, I already tried that and it only affected the timer. Let me try the blue block at rows 16-19 (above the first bar).
Wait, I also tried that earlier and it only affected the timer.
Let me reconsider the puzzle. Maybe the goal isn't to align the green segments. Maybe the goal is something else entirely.
Actually, let me look at the level from a different angle. In level 1, there was one bar and one object. The goal was to align the green segments. In level 2, there are two bars and one object. Maybe the goal is to align the object with BOTH bars, or maybe there's a second object I'm missing.
Let me check if there's anything on the first bar (rows 20-23).
Actually, looking at the segmentation, the first bar (Node 5) is all black with no colored segment. The second bar (Node 9) has the green segment.
Maybe the first bar is meant to have a green segment created by some action. Or maybe the goal is just to align with the second bar, and the first bar is a red herring.
Let me try a different approach. Maybe I need to move the object left again to get back to a position where I can move right more times. The object is currently at cols 24-25. If I move left once (to cols 20-21), then right again, maybe it can go further.
Actually, that doesn't make sense. The object was at cols 20-21 before and couldn't move right past that point.
Wait, but earlier the object was at cols 20-21 and I moved it left to cols 16-17, then right 3 times to cols 24-25. So the object CAN move past cols 20-21 when coming from the left. The issue is that from cols 24-25, it can't move further right.
Let me try moving left once and then right again to see if it can go further.
